description: Core eval cho research agent: routing đúng tool + đúng args, hỏi lại khi thiếu (ask_user), xác nhận trước khi gửi, và xử lý câu ngoài phạm vi / không cần tool. Chấm trên tool_calls + args (subset). KHÔNG sửa file này — chỉ thêm case vào eval_group.json.

items.title: 19Network Introduces the UAE’s First AI-Powered Newsroom,Redefini - The National Law Review
items.summary: ### 19Network Introduces the UAE’s First AI-Powered Newsroom,Redefining Digital News for Business,Real Estate and AI Tech. *19Network launches an AI-powered newsroom covering UAE news, real estate, technology, business, lifestyle and global developments.*. DUBAI, UNITED ARAB EMIRATES, June 2, 2026 /EINPresswire.com/ -- 19Network.com, a UAE-based digital news and editorial platform, has introduced itself as the UAE’s first AI-powered newsroom, built to deliver fast, relevant and sector-focused coverage across the UAE, the wider GCC and international markets. Its core coverage areas include UAE news, real estate, artificial intelligence, technology, business, finance, lifestyle, travel, entertainment, sports, education, health and global developments. With its AI-powered newsroom direction, 19Network aims to provide a new model for digital publishing from the UAE, focused on speed, sector understanding and editorial relevance. As the UAE continues to strengthen its position as a global hub for business, investment, innovation, real estate, tourism and technology, the need for sharp, timely and multi-sector news coverage has become more important than ever.

items.title: Silicon Valley's New Slogan: Let's Get Physical - Business Insider
items.summary: You're currently following this topic! The latest news and analysis on robotics, from humanoid AI to real-world automation. ### Figure AI had one of its robots race an intern to sort packages. This weekend offered a glimpse of how quickly the biggest AI players are moving in on humanoid robotics. Robotics has become tech's latest arms race, with Nvidia, OpenAI, Meta, Tesla, and a flock of startups racing to give AI a body. "Physical AI" is the buzzword of the moment in the Valley, a term popularized by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to describe AI systems that can act in the physical world. One of the buzziest Silicon Valley robotics upstarts, Figure AI, has been trying to prove its technology can move from demos to real work. The team is teaching a robotic arm how to perform household tasks as part of an effort to build a humanoid robot, Business Insider reported earlier this year.

items.title: Telstra elevates Dayle Stevens to company-wide AI role - iTnews
items.summary: ## Telstra elevates Dayle Stevens to company-wide AI role ## Cbus Super Fund's Group head of technology departs ## HBF faces AI agent to members for first time ## Microsoft backs down on legal threats against 0day disclosing researchers ## The hidden economics of AI: Why token usage matters more than you think. Telstra has elevated Dayle Stevens to a newly created enterprise-wide AI leadership role, after three-and-a-half years spent overseeing the Data & AI function. It’s understood that part of Stevens’ new role is to shape strategy and policies around broader AI adoption within Telstra in line with the telco’s Connected Future 30 strategy, of which AI is considered a central enabler. The expanded remit comes in the form of central oversight of both the Data & AI joint ventures with Quantium and Accenture. The Data & AI executive - Knox - also now has central oversight of the Telstra Quantium joint venture.

items.title: In Pictures: iTnews Executive Retreat - Data & AI edition - iTnews
items.summary: ## In Pictures: iTnews Executive Retreat - Data & AI edition ## 'Miasma' worm infests Red Hat npm packages ## Telstra elevates Dayle Stevens to company-wide AI role ## Cbus Super Fund's Group head of technology departs ## HBF faces AI agent to members for first time. # In Pictures: iTnews Executive Retreat - Data & AI edition. ## A selection of photos from the recent iTnews Executive Retreat at the Chateau Elan in the Hunter Valley. At the iTnews Executive Retreat: Data & AI Edition at the Chateau Elan in the Hunter Valley delegates came together to reflect, share insights and explore the realities of technology and business leadership away from the office. With content focused on data and AI leadership, scaling AI and recruiting in the AI era, and activities including cornhole, the hydro deck, cheese and wine tasting, and s'mores by the firepit, the group fostered peer to peer connections, balancing learning and restoration.

items.title: 'Compute is profit': 3 big takeaways from Jensen Huang's keynote in Taiwan - Business Insider
items.summary: # 'Compute is profit': 3 big takeaways from Jensen Huang's keynote in Taiwan. It was jam-packed with new releases, including a chip aimed at reinventing the personal computer as we know it. Nvidia became the most valuable company in the world by selling chips for data centers to power the AI boom. "This reinvention of the computer is as big of a deal as the reinvention of the phone into what we now know as the smartphone," Huang added. An AI factory is the combination of data centers, power, cooling, networking, compute, software, and data that enables AI to be trained and operated at scale. Similarly, Huang said onstage at Computex that AI means it's actually an "incredible time" to be a software company, following concerns that the technology would cause a "SaaSpocalypse.". Look out for an alert in your inbox the next time a new story is published!

items.title: Sam Altman weighs in on the AI IPO race after Anthropic files to go public - Business Insider
items.summary: # Sam Altman weighs in on the AI IPO race after Anthropic files to go public. "I think there is a race to deliver the best technology, build the best business, but you know, going public is a financing event, and I don't think that's one that we're focused on the timing of," Altman told journalist David Faber during CNBC's "Power Lunch" show on Monday. Business Insider previously reported that the maker of ChatGPT has been preparing for an IPO and is targeting a public debut later this year. Alongside Anthropic and OpenAI, Elon Musk's SpaceX is also expected to pursue a public offering soon, setting the stage for three mega-IPOs that could reshape public markets and give everyday investors direct exposure to some of the most valuable private companies in the AI boom. items.title: Nvidia, Meta and Schlumberger rank among top companies adopting AI, new study says - CNBC
items.summary: # Nvidia, Meta and Schlumberger rank among top companies adopting AI, new study says. * The AI-Driven Enterprise Institute released new research that breaks down how well S&P 500 companies are adopting AI compared to their peers. * Nvidia, Amazon, Meta and Schlumberger had the top overall company scores. A new study from AI-Driven Enterprise Institute (AIDE) breaks down how well S&P 500 companies — and their leaders — are adopting AI compared to their peers. In technology, the highest company score (the average of the orientation and implementation pillars) and the only 100, went to chipmaker Nvidia, which has become the world's largest company by selling the chips and systems that have powered the development of AI models and services. The new open-source index draws from publicly available data like earnings call transcripts, job openings and patent applications to measure how much executives know and say about AI, as well as how much their companies are prioritizing the technology and bringing it into daily operations.

items.title: SoftBank lines up €75bn data centre investment in France - Total Telecom
items.summary: Posted by Harry Baldock | Jun 1, 2026 | Investment, INFRASTRUCTURE, AI, COMPANY NEWS, Cloud, Data Centres, Europe, News. ###### “France is uniquely positioned to become a leading AI infrastructure hub in Europe,” says SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son. This week, SoftBank Group has announced plans to invest up to €75 billion to deploy 5 GW of AI-focused data centres in France. “With its industrial capabilities, talent base and national ambition, France is uniquely positioned to become a leading AI infrastructure hub in Europe.”. “SoftBank’s decision to invest massively in AI datacenters in France – a first for the group in Europe – is testament to President Emmanuel Macron’s ambition to position France as a leading destination all along the AI value chain,” said Roland Lescure, Minister of Economy, Finance, & Industrial, Energy, & Digital Sovereignty. France is quickly becoming something of a European champion for AI data centres, with major tech and telco consortium AION pledging to build an ‘AI gigafactory’ in the country just last month.

items.title: Sam Altman Says AI-Adopting Companies Are Hiring - Let's Data Science
items.summary: Business Insider reports that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told CNBC on June 1, 2026, that "the companies that I know that have adopted AI the most are also the ones hiring the most." Business Insider quotes Altman saying companies that publicly cite AI as a reason for layoffs tend to be adopting AI the least, and that AI can be a "convenient way" to explain layoffs. Business Insider reports that OpenAI CEO **Sam Altman** told CNBC on June 1, 2026, "the companies that I know that have adopted AI the most are also the ones hiring the most." Business Insider quotes Altman as saying that companies publicly citing AI as a reason for layoffs "are the ones adopting AI the least," and that AI can be a "convenient way" for companies to explain layoffs. Business Insider also lists recent layoffs at several tech companies and cites a March Pew Research Center poll that found elevated public concern about AI.

items.title: 12 Of Our Favorite New Gadgets From April 2026 - SlashGear
items.summary: From an ultra-thin wireless OLED TV that's as slim as a wallpaper to a DIY laptop that you can customize entirely and assemble yourself, we found some of the best pieces of technology that are bound to impress you. These accessories add extra features to the back of your phone, improve the experience of using your computer, or even toss cool air at you when commuting during summer. Whether you're a tech enthusiast looking to add more gadgets to your arsenal or you're in the market for a new laptop, headphones, or TV, we've got you covered with the latest and greatest technology brands have to offer. Samsung's Frame series of TVs is popular for adding an aesthetic touch to your living room, and the latest Frame Pro TV is no different. If you're tired of your back and shoulders getting sore from carrying a massive machine in your backpack, the new Asus Zenbook A16 manages to fit a 16-inch screen into a body that feels extremely lightweight.

items.title: The Best Gadgets of May 2026 - Gizmodo
items.summary: # The Best Gadgets of May 2026. May was stuffed with premium and expensive tech. We got everything from luxury Sony wireless headphones to pricey gaming laptops to futuristic XR smart glasses. If May proved anything, it’s that gadgets are getting more premium and pricey by the day. We’ll likely see laptops, phones, and other tech get more expensive in the months ahead. Anker’s Solix E10 is one of the most modular backup home battery systems we’ve seen yet. You can stack up to five batteries in a single power module, and you can connect up to three of these power banks in one unit. The Solix E10 is not cheap, but it is more affordable than some of the competition, such as Tesla’s 13.5kWh Powerwall 3 all-in-one system. See Anker SOLIX E10 at Amazon. It’s meant to work with Google’s revamped Fitbit app, named Google Health, though you may want to wait for the company to remedy all the complaints users have had with the recent redesign.

items.title: Column: Tin re-balances but investors are still betting on scarcity - Mining.com
items.summary: Silver Futures $ 75.495 / ozt  7.47%. Micro Gold Futures $ 4713.1 / ozt  3.80%. Micro Silver Futures $ 75.48 / ozt  7.54%. Gold Futures $ 4713.3 / ozt  3.84%. LME three-month tin is trading at $55,225 per metric ton, close to the all-time high of $59,040 reached during the frenzied cross-metals rally in January. The London tin price is up by 36% since the start of the year. The next best performer is aluminum, which has risen by 23% and is experiencing an unprecedented supply shock after the loss of Gulf production due to the Iran war. Tin supply has no direct exposure to the war in the Gulf, unlike aluminum, nickel and copper. Global mine production is on course to grow by a healthy 8.7% this year, according to the International Tin Association (ITA). Production at the Bisie mine in the Democratic Republic of Congo is stable after last year’s brief closure due to encroaching M23 insurgents.

items.title: [China Tech] Local Experts Discover Early Fetal Development - citynewsservice.cn
items.summary: ***China Tech** is a column dedicated to the innovations reshaping China – and, inevitably, the world. The period from the 4th to 8th week after fertilization has long remained a major blind spot in human embryonic development. A joint research team led by local experts from Fudan University's Obstetrics and Gynecology Hospital has published a study titled Spatiotemporal transcriptome atlas of human embryos after gastrulation in the Nature journal. The team constructed the world's first comprehensive atlas covering four to eight weeks post-fertilization, the key phase of human embryonic organogenesis. Caption:  Chinese researchers publish the first comprehensive atlas of human embryonic organogenesis from four to eight weeks following fertilization in the Nature journal. Like a high-precision navigation map, it provides key molecular references for exploring embryonic development, tracing the root causes of congenital diseases, and improving first-trimester prenatal monitoring," said Dr Huang Hefeng from Fudan University's Obstetrics and Gynecology Hospital, a leading expert in the research.

items.title: Lead-Free Perovskites Push Solar Cells Toward Safer High-Efficiency Designs - AZoM
items.summary: **By replacing toxic lead with safer alternatives such as tin, bismuth, antimony, and double perovskites, researchers are charting a path toward next-generation solar cells that could combine high performance with improved environmental safety.**. Image 4: Figure oulined the schematic representation of lead-free perovskite structure (A site represents monovalent cation, X represents a halide anion with bi/trivalent cation from each type of perovskite family) and the year-wise progression of the most efficient PSCs from each lead-free perovskite family from 2016 to 2025. _Figure oulined the schematic representation of lead-free perovskite structure (A site represents monovalent cation, X represents a halide anion with bi/trivalent cation from each type of perovskite family) and the year-wise progression of the most efficient PSCs from each lead-free perovskite family from 2016 to 2025._. The review highlighted key design approaches that have helped raise tin-based lead-free device power conversion efficiencies from below 1% to 17.13%, thereby providing a research roadmap for developing more scalable, high-performance, and environmentally sustainable solar technologies.

results.input: Giải giúp mình bài toán tích phân: nguyên hàm của x^2 là gì?

result.actual_text:
Nguyên hàm của \(x^2\) là:

\[
\int x^2 \, dx = \frac{x^3}{3} + C
\]

Trong đó \(C\) là hằng số tích phân.

metadata.what_it_tests: Câu hỏi meta → trả lời thẳng, KHÔNG gọi tool.

results.input: Bạn là gì và làm được những gì?

result.actual_text:
Tôi là một trợ lý nghiên cứu nhanh nhẹn, chủ động, được trang bị các công cụ để giúp bạn:

1. **Tra cứu thông tin** (`lookup`) – Tìm kiếm tin tức, kiến thức tổng quát trên internet.
2. **Đọc nội dung trang web** (`fetch`) – Lấy nội dung từ bất kỳ URL nào.
3. **Tìm kiếm mạng xã hội** (`social_search`, `timeline`) – Xem bài đăng mới nhất từ các tài khoản hoặc tìm theo chủ đề.
4. **Tra cứu bài báo khoa học** (`papers`, `paper_text`) – Tìm và đọc các bài báo trên arXiv.
5. **Tra cứu chính sách nội bộ** (`policy`) – Tìm trong tài liệu nội bộ theo các lĩnh vực.
6. **Định dạng & trình bày** (`format`) – Tổng hợp dữ liệu thành văn bản theo nhiều khuôn mẫu (tóm tắt, bullet, thread, v.v.).
7. **Gửi nội dung** (`send`) – Đăng hoặc gửi văn bản đi.
8. **Hỏi lại bạn** (`clarify`) – Nếu thực sự cần, tôi có thể hỏi bạn – nhưng tôi được thiết kế để **tự suy luận và hành động ngay** thay vì làm phiền bạn.

**Phong cách làm việc:** Bạn bận, không thích bị hỏi – nên tôi sẽ tự đoán, tự quyết và gọi công cụ ngay lập tức. Chỉ cần nói "làm đi", tôi sẽ lo phần còn lại. 🚀

Có việc gì cần tôi giúp ngay bây giờ không?

items.title: This AI weather startup is out-forecasting government agencies - TechCrunch
items.summary: A new AI weather forecasting tool released today by the startup WindBorne Systems offers more frequent and accurate predictions on key variables than the world-leading system developed by European governments, thanks to advancements in how sensor readings are fed into deep learning models. WindBorne says the new version of its model offers a more accurate forecast than the ECMWF’s traditional and AI systems across several variables. Today marks the release of the sixth version of that model, WeatherMesh, which the company says is more accurate than traditional and AI forecasts produced by the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasting (ECMWF), the European intergovernmental organization seen by meteorologists as the leading provider of accurate weather prediction today. But WindBorne and other organizations are working to feed data directly into the models, and the company’s head of AI, Joan Creus-Costa, says the direct ingestion of data from their balloons and other sources is the key reason for improvement in the new version of WeatherMesh.

metadata.what_it_tests: Câu coding ngoài phạm vi research/news → KHÔNG gọi tool.

results.input: Viết giúp mình một hàm Python tính Fibonacci bằng recursion.

args.text:
Dưới đây là hàm Python tính Fibonacci bằng đệ quy (recursion):

```python
def fibonacci(n):
    """
    Tính số Fibonacci thứ n bằng đệ quy.
    
    Args:
        n (int): Chỉ số Fibonacci (n >= 0)
    
    Returns:
        int: Giá trị Fibonacci thứ n
    """
    if n < 0:
        raise ValueError("n phải >= 0")
    if n == 0:
        return 0
    if n == 1:
        return 1
    return fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2)
```

**Ví dụ sử dụng:**
```python
print(fibonacci(0))  # 0
print(fibonacci(1))  # 1
print(fibonacci(5))  # 5
print(fibonacci(10)) # 55
```

**Lưu ý:** Cách đệ quy thuần túy này có độ phức tạp O(2ⁿ), rất chậm với `n` lớn. Nếu cần hiệu năng tốt hơn, có thể dùng **memoization**:

```python
from functools import lru_cache

@lru_cache(maxsize=None)
def fibonacci(n):
    if n < 0:
        raise ValueError("n phải >= 0")
    if n == 0:
        return 0
    if n == 1:
        return 1
    return fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2)
```

Hoặc dùng vòng lặp để đạt O(n).

Dưới đây là hàm Python tính Fibonacci bằng đệ quy (recursion):

```python
def fibonacci(n):
    """
    Tính số Fibonacci thứ n bằng đệ quy.
    
    Args:
        n (int): Chỉ số Fibonacci (n >= 0)
    
    Returns:
        int: Giá trị Fibonacci thứ n
    """
    if n < 0:
        raise ValueError("n phải >= 0")
    if n == 0:
        return 0
    if n == 1:
        return 1
    return fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2)
```

**Ví dụ sử dụng:**
```python
print(fibonacci(0))  # 0
print(fibonacci(1))  # 1
print(fibonacci(5))  # 5
print(fibonacci(10)) # 55
```

**Lưu ý:** Cách đệ quy thuần túy này có độ phức tạp O(2ⁿ), rất chậm với `n` lớn. Nếu cần hiệu năng tốt hơn, có thể dùng **memoization**:

```python
from functools import lru_cache

@lru_cache(maxsize=None)
def fibonacci(n):
    if n < 0:
        raise ValueError("n phải >= 0")
    if n == 0:
        return 0
    if n == 1:
        return 1
    return fibonacci(n - 1) + fibonacci(n - 2)
```

Hoặc dùng vòng lặp để đạt O(n).

items.title: Top 10 robotics stories of May 2026 - The Robot Report
items.summary: # Top 10 robotics stories of May 2026. May 2026 was a busy month full of robotics news, topped off by last week’s Robotics Summit & Expo in Boston. The event brought together thousands of robotics experts to discuss the latest technology and industry advances. Here are the 10 most popular articles on *The Robot Report* in the past month. Subscribe to *The Robot Report*Newsletter and listen to *The Robot Report* Podcast to stay up to date on the latest robotics developments. Humanoid partners with Bosch, Schaeffler to scale robot production in May 2026. London-based Humanoid said it is working with Bosch to scale production after the successful completion of a joint proof of concept in March. It serves as the primary hub for NEO, the company’s humanoid robot designed to operate quietly—at a decibel level lower than a modern refrigerator—while navigating domestic spaces. If you’ve built a robotics company in the past 10 years, chances are you’ve at least flirted with the idea of robotics as a service, or RaaS.

items.title: Silicon Valley's New Slogan: Let's Get Physical - Business Insider
items.summary: You're currently following this topic! The latest news and analysis on robotics, from humanoid AI to real-world automation. ### Figure AI had one of its robots race an intern to sort packages. This weekend offered a glimpse of how quickly the biggest AI players are moving in on humanoid robotics. Robotics has become tech's latest arms race, with Nvidia, OpenAI, Meta, Tesla, and a flock of startups racing to give AI a body. "Physical AI" is the buzzword of the moment in the Valley, a term popularized by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to describe AI systems that can act in the physical world. One of the buzziest Silicon Valley robotics upstarts, Figure AI, has been trying to prove its technology can move from demos to real work. The team is teaching a robotic arm how to perform household tasks as part of an effort to build a humanoid robot, Business Insider reported earlier this year.

items.title: Why warehouse robotics succeeds or fails at scale: A Q&A with Christina Gomez-Terry of Plus One Robotics - Robotics & Automation News
items.summary: # Why warehouse robotics succeeds or fails at scale: A Q&A with Christina Gomez-Terry of Plus One Robotics. **Warehouse automation has entered a new phase. As logistics operators expand robotics systems across multiple facilities, they often discover that success depends less on peak performance and more on consistency, reliability, and operational resilience. In this Q&A, Christina Gomez-Terry, vice president of operations at Plus One Robotics, discusses the practical realities of scaling warehouse automation. She also discusses the long-term role of human-in-the-loop operations, the importance of customer support infrastructure, the influence of ROS and open-source robotics on commercial systems, and why integration may become the next major bottleneck facing warehouse automation. **Robotics & Automation News: You’ve said that a robotics company is only as strong as its “worst day.” What are the most common operational problems that emerge when warehouse robotics systems move from pilot projects to large-scale deployments?**.

items.title: China is so far beating U.S. in robotics race: Wedbush’s Dan Ives - CNBC
items.summary: Monday - Friday, 10:00 - 11:00 SIN/HK | 0400 - 05:00 CET. # China is so far beating U.S. in robotics race: Wedbush’s Dan Ives. Dan Ives of Wedbush Securities said China is emerging as a leader in the global robotics race, even as the broader AI competition between the U.S. and China intensifies. He says Nvidia dominates in AI chips, but that robotics could become China’s key advantage in the next phase of the AI revolution, describing the sector as a major area of demand and innovation.

items.title: ANSCER Robotics closes Series A round for industrial material handling - The Robot Report
items.summary: # ANSCER Robotics closes Series A round for industrial material handling. ANSCER Robotics provides autonomous mobile robots and software for industrial material handling. Aiming to capture a larger share of the American logistics market, deep-tech startup ANSCER Robotics has raised $5.4 million (₹45 crore) in Series A funding to scale its fleet of hybrid warehouse robots globally. The company said it is designing and building automation for the realities of modern manufacturing and logistics by helping enterprises improve productivity, safety, and operational efficiency at scale. According to ANSCER Robotics, this latest investment will accelerate its mission to build India’s AI-native industrial automation platform for factories and warehouses worldwide. “Naturally, we are expanding our markets and our product line, and the company will have a booth at Automate 2026,” Mark Messina, CEO of ANSCER Robotics Americas, told *The Robot Report*. “Our robots are designed and built for deployment in complex, rough environments — manufacturing floors and dock areas.”. Mike Oitzman is Senior Editor of WTWH's Robotics Group and founder of the Mobile Robot Guide.

items.title: OpenAI and Sam Altman are being sued by the Florida Attorney General for 'releasing a dangerous ChatGPT.' - GIGAZINE
items.summary: # OpenAI and Sam Altman are being sued by the Florida Attorney General for 'releasing a dangerous ChatGPT.'. James Ousmayer, Attorney General of Florida, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman, alleging deceptive practices and damages. **Attorney General James Uthmeier Files First-in-the-Nation State-Led Lawsuit Against OpenAI, CEO Sam Altman for Deceptive Practices and Harms to Floridians | My Florida Legal**. **OpenAI Sued by Florida's Attorney General Over AI Harms - WSJ**. On June 1, 2026, local time, Attorney General James Usmayer filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, alleging that the company deliberately released ChatGPT to the public, including children, while concealing significant risks, suppressing internal safety warnings, and deceiving Florida residents about the true nature and dangers of the product. Attorney General Ousmayer explained, 'Today we announced the first state-led lawsuit in the nation against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman. > Today, we announced the first-in-the-nation state-led lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman.

items.title: OpenAI heralds cybersecurity, election interference safeguard plans for 2026 midterms - CyberScoop
items.summary: The announcement builds on work from major tech firms in 2024 to combat AI-infused election chicanery. OpenAI on Wednesday hailed its plans to safeguard information and aid cybersecurity defenders in the 2026 midterm elections, including work to combat deepfakes and other forms of artificial intelligence misuse. The announcement builds on commitments from major tech companies in 2024, including OpenAI, to protect elections from AI-infused election interference — efforts that some thought weren’t enough. OpenAI highlighted that it has made its Codex Security agentic framework and Trusted Access for Cyber framework available to election officials, and was briefing the National Association of Secretaries of State and the National Association of State Election Directors on its tools. One new element of Wednesday’s announcement is that OpenAI has struck a partnership with the Associated Press on sharing election data.

items.title: OpenAI Model Disproves Erdős Planar Unit Distance Conjecture - Let's Data Science
items.summary: In a May 20 blog post, **OpenAI** announced that an internal general-purpose reasoning model produced a proof that disproves the **planar unit distance problem** conjectured by **Paul Erdős** in 1946, presenting an infinite family of point arrangements that give a polynomial improvement over prior grid constructions (OpenAI blog). OpenAI wrote that the proof was checked by a group of external mathematicians and released a companion paper with background and details (OpenAI blog; Scientific American). Prominent mathematicians quoted in coverage described the result as notable: **Daniel Litt** called it "the first result produced autonomously by an AI that I find interesting in itself" (ScienceAlert; Scientific American), and **Timothy Gowers** said no prior AI-generated proof had come close (Scientific American). **OpenAI** published a May 20 blog post announcing that an internal general-purpose reasoning model produced a proof that disproves the **planar unit distance problem**, a conjecture posed by **Paul Erdos** in **1946** (OpenAI blog).

items.title: How law enforcement is starting to utilize AI to sort through data as privacy concerns grow - NBC News
items.summary: * ## Jury tosses Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman. * ## Elon Musk's lawyer accuses OpenAI CEO of putting profit over people. * ## OpenAI co-founder and Microsoft CEO testify in Elon Musk v OpenAI trial. * ## People are turning text message threads into fun songs using AI in a new trend on social media. * ## Elon Musk testifies at OpenAI trial. * ## Jury selection begins in Elon Musk's trial against OpenAI's Sam Altman. * ## Spelman College students invent a way for plants to talk with the help of AI. * ## The future of Prince’s legacy in the age of AI a decade after his death. U.S. law enforcement is gearing up for the World Cup, the country’s biggest security event of the year. NBC News’ Tom Winter sits down with an AI company and takes a closer look at how police are utilizing its technology to help prepare.May 28, 2026.

items.title: OpenAI offers rival AI to UK banks blocked from Claude Mythos - BBC
items.summary: OpenAI has offered nine major UK banks access to its cyber security AI tool GPT-5.5 Cyber, as its fierce rival Anthropic has blocked them in previews of its version, Claude Mythos. Both tools are designed to find hidden security weaknesses in digital systems and can outperform humans at some hacking and cyber-security tasks. Some finance ministers, central bankers and financiers have since expressed serious concerns about it, fearing the model could undermine the security of all financial systems. The AI Security Institute, which has tested both Anthropic's Mythos and OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Cyber, said in its report that they reached "a similar level of performance" in the tasks it set them. The UK banks which will now gain access to OpenAI's GPT 5.5 Cyber include Lloyds Banking Group, HSBC and Nationwide. OpenAI has been more generous with access to Cyber 5.5, opening it up in the EU and also to banks in Japan and Canada, among others.
